jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with Homer presumably getting into an accident
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - iconic but routine by this point in the series

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Lance Murdock jumping 16 blazing school buses then saying 'I'm goin' to Duff Gardens!'
jokes.setup: Daredevil completes dangerous stunt, interviewer asks what he'll do now
jokes.punchline: Instead of Disneyland parody, he's going to beer-themed park
jokes.explanation: Solid parody of Disney commercial formula with alcohol twist - well-structured setup/payoff

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.line: So Lance, you've just completed that death-defying stunt. What are you going to do now?

dialogue.speaker: Lance Murdock
dialogue.line: I'm going to Bud Gardens!

jokes.text: To be completed in 1994. [Groaning, Screams]
jokes.setup: Whiplash ride being advertised
jokes.punchline: Ride isn't even finished but people are already getting hurt on it
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about unsafe amusement park construction - economical and well-timed

dialogue.speaker: Announcer
dialogue.line: Welcome to the Whiplash ride! The most thrilling experience of your life!

dialogue.speaker: Riders
dialogue.line: Ahhhhhhh!

dialogue.speaker: Announcer
dialogue.line: The ride isn't even finished yet, but people are already getting hurt on it.

jokes.text: Now, what do we say when we get to the ticket booth? 'We're under six.' And I'm a college student.
jokes.setup: Homer coaching kids on lying for cheaper admission
jokes.punchline: Homer plans to lie about being a college student for discount
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - childish and scheming for trivial gain

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Now kids, to get the student discount, you just have to tell them you're in college.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: But Dad, we're in elementary school.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I know. That's why it's called lying. Now, I'm going in as a grad student.

jokes.text: 'Bout yay high, blue hair, big dent in her forehead? No, honey. Gladys looked more like your Aunt Patty.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to remember Great-Aunt Gladys by unflattering description
jokes.punchline: Marge corrects him that Gladys looked like Patty (implying Patty is attractive)
jokes.explanation: Homer's tactless description contrasted with Marge's diplomatic correction

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Great-Aunt Gladys? You mean that big, mannish woman with the deep voice and the five o'clock shadow?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, Gladys looked like Patty.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, that's right.

jokes.text: [Shudders] Oh, yeah. There she is.
jokes.setup: Homer realizing Gladys looked like Patty
jokes.punchline: Homer's disgusted reaction to remembering what she looked like
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that lands the previous joke about Patty's appearance

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait a minute... Gladys looked like Patty? Oh no, no, no, no, no! Bleccchhh!

jokes.text: But I wanna go to Duff Gardens right now! Oh, Homer, quit pouting. I'm not pouting. I'm mourning. Stupid dead woman.
jokes.setup: Homer whining about missing Duff Gardens, Marge calling him out
jokes.punchline: Homer's weak excuse followed by insulting the deceased
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer childishness escalating to inappropriate anger at dead relative

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But Marge, I miss Duff Gardens!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, you went there last weekend.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, but my memory's not what it used to be. I can barely remember what happened last week.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: You literally just said you went there last weekend.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I know, but I've been so sad since they closed down the Duff beer statue of that dead guy they had there.

jokes.text: We're hugging us. What do we do? Just close your eyes and think of MacGyver.
jokes.setup: Patty and Selma uncomfortable with Homer hugging them at funeral
jokes.punchline: Selma's advice references her crush on MacGyver to get through unpleasant experience
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific joke that references Selma's established MacGyver obsession in perfect context

dialogue.speaker: Patty
dialogue.line: Oh, this is so uncomfortable.

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Just think of it like MacGyver trapped in a cave. He'd find a way out.

jokes.text: Hey, you don't hear Lisa complaining. [Muffled Whimpering]
jokes.setup: Homer defending himself about luggage crushing Bart
jokes.punchline: Lisa is actually being crushed worse but can't speak
jokes.explanation: Dark physical comedy with perfect timing - Homer's obliviousness contrasted with Lisa's suffering

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I didn't crush Bart! The luggage did!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad, I'm the one being crushed...

jokes.text: [Thinking] Yeah. The legend of the dog-faced woman. [Laughing] Legend of the dog-faced woman. Oh, that's good!
jokes.setup: Patty talking about Gladys's legend living forever
jokes.punchline: Homer thinking cruel thoughts about dead woman's appearance and congratulating himself
jokes.explanation: Homer's inappropriate thoughts at funeral - character consistent but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Patty
dialogue.line: Gladys's legend will live forever.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, too bad her face won't. *chuckles* Whoa, I'm a bad man.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. That was Prince of Tides.
jokes.setup: Marge seemingly giving heartfelt eulogy about Great-Aunt Gladys
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was actually reciting plot of a movie
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - builds emotional moment then deflates with pop culture reference. Perfect structure.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Great-Aunt Gladys was a woman of remarkable strength and character. She overcame tremendous adversity, and in the end, she found love and redemption.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That was beautiful, Marge.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Thanks. I got it from the back of the DVD case for 'Steel Magnolias.'

jokes.text: That's a woman? Dear Lord! Well, I guess most of what I said can be salvaged.
jokes.setup: Minister giving eulogy for 'good man' when corrected that deceased is a woman
jokes.punchline: Minister's awkward attempt to salvage inappropriate eulogy
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - minister's professional composure cracking under embarrassing mistake

dialogue.speaker: Minister
dialogue.line: He was a good man, a fine man, a man among men...

dialogue.speaker: Attendee
dialogue.line: She was a woman.

dialogue.speaker: Minister
dialogue.line: She was a good man.

jokes.text: Take a seat, bozo. And that eulogy better not show up on the bill.
jokes.setup: Minister making mistake at funeral
jokes.punchline: Patty's characteristically harsh response and practical concern about payment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patty character moment - rude and cheap, exactly what we expect

dialogue.speaker: Minister
dialogue.line: Ashes to ashes, dust to dust... we commit this body to the... to the... well, to wherever dead people go.

dialogue.speaker: Patty
dialogue.line: Yeah, real inspiring, Reverend. Say, who's paying for this thing? Because if it's coming out of the estate, we're gonna need to talk about my fee.

jokes.text: Gladys lived alone, died alone. I guess you could say she was a role model for Selma and me.
jokes.setup: Patty giving eulogy for Aunt Gladys
jokes.punchline: Describing lonely death as aspirational role model
jokes.explanation: Dark humor perfectly matched to Patty's cynical character - finding inspiration in isolation

dialogue.speaker: Patty
dialogue.line: Aunt Gladys lived a life of solitude and quiet desperation. She died alone in her apartment, and wasn't found for three weeks. And you know what? That's the dream. That's what I'm working toward.

jokes.text: Boy, am I hungry. I mean I'm really, really hungry. It's just not fair, damn it! [Sobbing]
jokes.setup: Homer at funeral thinking about food
jokes.punchline: His hunger escalates to emotional breakdown at inappropriate time
jokes.explanation: Homer's selfish priorities even at funeral - good character consistency with escalating emotion

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... funeral potatoes...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, are those little weenies in a blanket?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: No, Homer, focus. This is a solemn occasion.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But they look so good...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why would they put the food right there if they didn't want us to—

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: *stomach growls loudly*

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why?! Why would they do this to me?! *breaks down crying* I'm so hungry and so sad!

jokes.text: [Throaty Voice] Don't worry about it. [Screaming]
jokes.setup: Lisa giving sincere goodbye to Great-Aunt Gladys
jokes.punchline: Bart scaring her by pretending to be the dead woman's voice
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart prank on Lisa - character appropriate but fairly predictable setup

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Great-Aunt Gladys, I just want you to know how much you meant to all of us. You were a wonderful woman and we'll miss you so much.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ooooooh, Lisa... it's me, Great-Aunt Gladys, speaking from beyond the grave...

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart!

jokes.text: She left a video will, so I earn my fee simply by pressing this 'Play' button. Pretty sweet, hey?
jokes.setup: Lionel Hutz as executor of estate
jokes.punchline: His pride in earning money for minimal work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lionel Hutz character moment - sleazy lawyer proud of easy money

dialogue.speaker: Lionel Hutz
dialogue.line: As executor of this estate, I've taken the liberty of transferring all assets to my personal account. Minimal effort, maximum compensation. That's the Lionel Hutz guarantee.

jokes.text: All in favor of skipping the poem. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Homer fast-forwarding through Robert Frost poem in video will
jokes.punchline: Homer's impatience with culture, treating solemn moment like democracy
jokes.explanation: Homer's cultural impatience and inappropriate democratic approach to funeral proceedings

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, why do I have to watch this? It's a guy reading poetry.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, it's Robert Frost. It's culturally important.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Important? The only thing important is my finger on this fast-forward button.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Two roads diverged in a wood? I diverge to the fridge!

jokes.text: To my executor, Lionel Hutz I leave $50,000. Mr. Hutz! You'd be surprised how often that works. You really would.
jokes.setup: Video will stating large sum for Hutz, family reacts
jokes.punchline: Hutz admits he tries this scam frequently and it sometimes works
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Hutz moment - admits to trying fraud and expresses genuine surprise it sometimes works. Perfect character writing.

dialogue.speaker: Lionel Hutz
dialogue.line: Well, I've tried this scam before. Sometimes it works!

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: You've tried this before?!

dialogue.speaker: Lionel Hutz
dialogue.line: Oh sure, all the time. You'd be surprised how often people fall for it.

jokes.text: These chips were my children, Marjorie. Take special care of them. [Crunching] Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Gladys emotionally bequeathing celebrity-shaped potato chip collection
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately starts eating them during the will reading
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition of sentimental value vs Homer's appetite - perfect timing with sound effect

dialogue.speaker: Gladys
dialogue.line: I leave my celebrity-shaped potato chip collection to Homer Simpson.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... celebrity chips.

dialogue.speaker: Lawyer
dialogue.line: Mr. Simpson, perhaps you should wait until after the will reading is complete.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Can't stop. Eating. Chips.

jokes.text: Why didn't she just leave me the bowel obstruction that killed her?
jokes.setup: Selma inheriting pet iguana JubJub
jokes.punchline: Comparing unwanted pet inheritance to literal cause of death
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - equating pet care burden with fatal medical condition. Very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: I inherited JubJub from my sister. It's like inheriting her problems.

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: At least the iguana won't kill you.

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: No, but neglect might.

jokes.text: Don't die lonely like me. Raise a family, and do it now. Now! Now! Now!
jokes.setup: Gladys giving advice to Patty and Selma about not dying alone
jokes.punchline: The echoing repetition making it sound desperate and haunting
jokes.explanation: The echoing effect adds absurdist element to otherwise straightforward advice scene

dialogue.speaker: Gladys
dialogue.line: You don't want to die alone. You don't want to die alone. You don't want to die alone.

jokes.text: Don't worry. We'll get that 'barking dog' record tomorrow. Patty, I want a baby.
jokes.setup: Selma saying something is missing in their lives
jokes.punchline: Patty misinterprets as wanting an annoying sound effect record, then Selma drops baby bombshell
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Patty's literal interpretation before Selma's emotional revelation

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: You know, Patty, I think something's missing in our lives.

dialogue.speaker: Patty
dialogue.line: You mean like this record of annoying sound effects I just picked up?

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: No, I mean a baby.

dialogue.speaker: Patty
dialogue.line: A baby?!

jokes.text: You're lookin' at a free lunch, boys. Come and get it. [Imitates Tiger Snarl]
jokes.setup: Selma at dating service describing herself to potential matches
jokes.punchline: Her aggressive, unsexy sales pitch with animal noises
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selma character moment - desperate and completely lacking in seduction skills

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Hello, I'm Selma Bouvier. I'm looking for a man who enjoys the finer things in life - like watching me eat, and listening to me complain about my job at the DMV.

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: I have many interests: collecting cats, practicing my seal call, and telling men why they're not good enough for me.

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: *makes aggressive barking and seal sounds* Arooooo! Eek eek eek!

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: So, any takers?

jokes.text: [Scottish Accent] Ach! Back to the loch with you, Nessie.
jokes.setup: Selma trying to be seductive on dating video
jokes.punchline: Worker's Scottish accent comparing her to Loch Ness Monster
jokes.explanation: Cruel but funny comparison with specific accent choice adding flavor

dialogue.speaker: Selma Bouvier
dialogue.line: I'm Selma Bouvier, and I'm looking for a man who appreciates the finer things... like moxie.

dialogue.speaker: Dating Service Worker
dialogue.line: Aye, ye know, she reminds me of the Loch Ness Monster.

dialogue.speaker: Dating Service Worker
dialogue.line: People say they've seen her, but nobody's got any proof.

jokes.text: Mostly corn syrup, a little rubbing alcohol. You'll be lucky if it doesn't make your hair fall out actually.
jokes.setup: Selma asking about magical ingredients in love potion
jokes.punchline: Fortune teller admits it's fake and potentially harmful
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of mystical nonsense with practical harmful ingredients

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: What magical ingredients are in this love potion?

dialogue.speaker: Gypsy Fortune Teller
dialogue.line: Well, it's not really magical. And it might be a little bit poisonous.

jokes.text: So, wearing a belt, are ya? Yep. No suspenders for you. I guess not.
jokes.setup: Selma trying to make conversation with grocery store clerk
jokes.punchline: Painfully awkward small talk about clothing choices
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe comedy showing Selma's complete lack of social skills in dating attempts

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Nice vest. Where'd you get it?

dialogue.speaker: Store Clerk
dialogue.line: It came with the job.

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, it really brings out the... fluorescent in your complexion.

dialogue.speaker: Store Clerk
dialogue.line: ...Thanks.

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: I'm thinking of getting one myself. Do you think they come in my size?

dialogue.speaker: Store Clerk
dialogue.line: We're not hiring.

jokes.text: I'm not allowed to date customers. It's store policy. No, it isn't. Ow! Shut up, Arnold.
jokes.setup: Clerk rejecting Selma citing store policy
jokes.punchline: Co-worker corrects him, gets told to shut up, revealing the real reason
jokes.explanation: Standard rejection comedy with workplace dynamics adding layer

dialogue.speaker: Store Clerk
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, I can't serve you. It's store policy.

dialogue.speaker: Co-worker
dialogue.line: Actually, there is no such policy.

dialogue.speaker: Store Clerk
dialogue.line: Shut up, you're ruining this for me.

jokes.text: Cold biscuits... chicken... yellow... mailman. You're reading the wine list, sir.
jokes.setup: Selma on date apparently describing romantic feelings
jokes.punchline: She's actually just reading wine list randomly
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke - builds romantic expectation then deflates with mundane reality

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: You know, when I look into your eyes, I see a certain... je ne sais quoi. A certain indefinable quality that makes my heart skip a beat.

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: That's very sweet.

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Pinot Grigio, 1987. Subtle notes of pear and elderflower. Pairs well with seafood or poultry.

jokes.text: Ew! Get outta my car.
jokes.setup: Selma presumably making physical advance in car
jokes.punchline: Date's disgusted rejection
jokes.explanation: Simple rejection beat - functional but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Selma's Date
dialogue.line: Ugh! Get off me, you swine!

jokes.text: where roaming gangs aren't a big problem anymore.
jokes.setup: Duff Gardens commercial promoting family fun
jokes.punchline: Admitting they used to have gang violence problems
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in theme park advertising - suggesting crime was recently a problem

dialogue.speaker: Announcer
dialogue.line: Duff Gardens: where family fun is our priority. We've completely eliminated our gang violence problems.

jokes.text: Aunt Selma, this may be presumptuous but have you ever considered artificial insemination? Boy, I don't know. You gotta be pretty desperate to make it with a robot.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting artificial insemination to help Selma have baby
jokes.punchline: Selma misunderstanding and thinking it involves robots
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy - Selma's ignorance about medical procedures with robotic confusion

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Artificial insemination is a safe and proven way to have a baby.

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Robots?! But I'm afraid of robots!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: No, no, Selma. Artificial insemination. It's a medical procedure.

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Oh thank God. I thought you were suggesting I let some cold, heartless machine father my child.

jokes.text: All done! Thank you. Always a pleasure. What a beautiful little girl. [Loud Belch]
jokes.setup: Worker commenting on beautiful baby
jokes.punchline: Baby immediately belches loudly, undermining the compliment
jokes.explanation: Simple subversion of 'beautiful baby' moment with crude sound effect

dialogue.speaker: Sperm Bank Worker
dialogue.line: What a beautiful baby!

dialogue.speaker: Baby
dialogue.line: *BELCH*

jokes.text: A Nobel Prize winner! An NBA All-Star! Ooh! One of the Sweathogs. I checked. It's not Horshack.
jokes.setup: Selma reading impressive sperm donor credentials
jokes.punchline: Building up to TV show reference, then disappointing specification it's not the popular character
jokes.explanation: Excellent cultural reference joke with perfect deflation - Horshack was the fan favorite Sweathog

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Oh my goodness, look at these credentials! Ivy League education, athletic, artistic... This donor is perfect!

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Wait, let me see who this is... Oh! It's like that handsome doctor from that TV show!

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: McDreamy from Grey's Anatomy! No wait... it says here his name is... Moe Szyslak.

jokes.text: Ooh! You look just like your picture.
jokes.setup: Someone knocking at door, Selma expecting mail-order husband
jokes.punchline: Implying the person looks terrible since we know how Selma looks
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier catalog ordering joke with implication about mutual attractiveness

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Oh boy, my mail-order husband is finally here!

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Hello there, you must be...

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you're hideous!

jokes.text: I got a lotta love to give and, right now, my only outlet is my ham radio.
jokes.setup: Selma explaining why she wants a baby
jokes.punchline: Her current emotional outlet is impersonal radio hobby
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - unexpected hobby that emphasizes her loneliness

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: I want a baby because my life needs meaning. My current emotional outlet is an impersonal radio hobby.

jokes.text: No man will want you. All I got now is sperm in a cup.
jokes.setup: Marge warning Selma about consequences of having baby
jokes.punchline: Selma's brutally honest assessment of her current romantic prospects
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - Selma's matter-of-fact acceptance of her dating situation

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Oh, what are the chances I'll ever find a man willing to date me anyway?

jokes.text: Oh, great. Dad's dead.
jokes.setup: Homer lying motionless, presumably from food poisoning
jokes.punchline: Lisa's casual assumption and matter-of-fact delivery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa deadpan - treating family crisis with resigned acceptance

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Dad's dead.

jokes.text: Geez, we hardly made a dent in that 10-foot hoagie. Well, I'll give it a good home. Mm!
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on leftover giant sandwich
jokes.punchline: Homer's noble self-sacrifice to 'give it a good home' by eating it
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer rationalization for gluttony - framing overconsumption as charity

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, we have this giant sandwich left over from the party. What are we going to do with it?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Don't worry, Marge. I'll give it a good home.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... noble self-sacrifice.

jokes.text: Two more feet, and I can fit it in the fridge.
jokes.setup: Homer determined to finish eating massive sandwich
jokes.punchline: His bizarre logic that eating more will solve storage problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - eating more food to create space. Perfect character absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I can't store this sandwich anywhere. I know! I'll eat more of it!

jokes.text: Marge, I'd like to be alone with the sandwich for a moment. Are you going to eat it? Yes.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for private moment, suggesting romantic intimacy
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical question and Homer's honest answer
jokes.explanation: Great setup implying romantic moment with food, deflated by practical exchange

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, how about we go upstairs and make love?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, it's the middle of the afternoon.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: So?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, don't you have to be at work?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I called in sick.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: But you were just at work this morning.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I know, but I'm feeling much worse now.

jokes.text: To get to Duff Gardens, I'd ride with Satan himself. That's the spirit.
jokes.setup: Marge thanking Selma for taking kids to Duff Gardens
jokes.punchline: Bart's extreme statement and Marge's inappropriate encouragement
jokes.explanation: Good Bart hyperbole with Marge's oblivious positive response

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Aunt Selma took us to Duff Gardens and I drank so much Duff I peed myself!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, that's just a sign you're having fun!

jokes.text: This is all your fault! It-- Oh, how can I stay mad at you?
jokes.setup: Homer angry at sandwich for making him sick
jokes.punchline: Immediately forgiving the inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer relationship with food - treating sandwich like romantic partner

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: This sandwich made me sick! I'm gonna destroy it!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, who am I kidding? I forgive you, sandwich. We've been through so much together.

jokes.text: Twenty-two immigrant laborers died during its construction. Plenty more where that came from.
jokes.setup: Informational sign about Duff Beeramid construction deaths
jokes.punchline: Selma's callous dismissal of human casualties
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about corporate callousness toward immigrant workers - very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Sign
dialogue.line: Duff Beeramid construction deaths: 60

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: That's not too bad.

jokes.text: Hey! Take a picture. It'll last longer. Get outta here.
jokes.setup: Kids excited to see Seven Duffs characters
jokes.punchline: Surly living up to his name with hostile response to fans
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor - Surly acting exactly as name suggests

dialogue.speaker: Surly
dialogue.line: Get outta here! All of ya! I don't got time for this! You think I'm gonna stand around signin' autographs for every slack-jawed fan that wanders by? Beat it!

jokes.text: This is a disgrace. Settle down. Anything this bad has to be educational.
jokes.setup: Selma complaining about poor quality attraction
jokes.punchline: Bart's sarcastic logic that terrible quality equals educational value
jokes.explanation: Good cynical observation about educational entertainment being low quality

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: This attraction is terrible! The animatronics are broken, the story makes no sense, and it smells like feet.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Yeah, but think about it—the worse something is, the more you learn from it. Like, you're learning what NOT to do. So technically, this is the most educational place ever.

jokes.text: What are the odds of getting sick on a Saturday? 1,000-to-1.
jokes.setup: Homer calculating odds of weekend illness
jokes.punchline: His completely made-up and oddly specific statistic
jokes.explanation: Homer's fake expertise with specific but meaningless statistics

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Let's see... if I eat the expired clams, the probability of getting sick this weekend is... *pulls out calculator* ...73.6 percent. Those are pretty good odds!

jokes.text: Yentl? What's that? It deals with a bookish young woman's efforts to enter rabbinical school. Sounds great! Oh, my God! You're delirious.
jokes.setup: Marge describing serious artistic film to Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer showing interest in intellectual content, Marge recognizing this as sign of illness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character joke - Homer's interest in culture as medical symptom. Perfect setup/payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: It's a film about the alienation of modern man, shot entirely in black and white with subtitles.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hmm, that sounds interesting.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, you're scaring me. I'm calling Dr. Hibbert.

jokes.text: You're charming the pants off of me. What did you say, Aunt Selma? I said take off those damn glasses!
jokes.setup: Bart wearing beer goggles that make Selma look attractive
jokes.punchline: Revealing Selma's actual harsh response vs. what the goggles made Bart hear
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag concept with clear setup/payoff structure about perception vs reality

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Selma, you're looking great tonight. How about we go somewhere?

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Oh Bart, I'd love to, but I'm afraid I have cats to feed and a very busy evening of eating alone planned.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: That's the sweetest thing I've ever heard.

jokes.text: Get bent.
jokes.setup: Someone commenting about line being long so ride must be good
jokes.punchline: Surly's characteristically rude response
jokes.explanation: Consistent character beat - Surly maintaining his surly personality

dialogue.speaker: Surly
dialogue.line: Oh, the line's long, so the ride must be good? That's the dumbest thing I've ever heard. Long lines just mean more people are waiting to be disappointed. Now get outta my way before I make you part of the ride.

jokes.text: That Yentl puts the 'she' in yeshiva. Hmm. Well, you've cooled down. That's what you think. [Imitates Tiger Growl]
jokes.setup: Homer making wordplay about Yentl and yeshiva
jokes.punchline: Marge thinks fever broke, but Homer's tiger growl suggests otherwise
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay followed by Homer's continued delirium despite appearing better

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm like Yentl at a yeshiva!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Your fever broke!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: *growls like a tiger*

jokes.text: The Erotic Adventures of Hercules with Norman Fell as Zeus. Whoo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Marge offering alternative movie for sick Homer
jokes.punchline: Casting Norman Fell (Mr. Roper from Three's Company) as Zeus in adult film
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd casting choice - Norman Fell's mundane persona as Greek god in adult film

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: How about this one? It's called 'Busty Greek Maidens.'

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Eh, I dunno...

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: It stars Norman Fell as Zeus.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Norman Fell?! Sold!

jokes.text: I wanna get off! You can't get off. We have five more continents to visit.
jokes.setup: Lisa trapped on terrible Small World-style ride
jokes.punchline: Operator's indifferent response about extended torture duration
jokes.explanation: Standard trapped-on-ride comedy with institutional indifference

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: How long is this ride?

dialogue.speaker: Ride Operator
dialogue.line: Fifteen minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Fifteen minutes?!

dialogue.speaker: Ride Operator
dialogue.line: Yeah, but it feels like hours.

jokes.text: They're all around me! No way out! No way out, I tell you!
jokes.setup: Lisa increasingly disturbed by animatronic dolls
jokes.punchline: Her complete psychological breakdown
jokes.explanation: Standard 'Small World' parody breakdown - predictable but effective

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: These animatronic dolls are deeply unsettling. The uncanny valley of their artificial smiles, the jerky movements... it's like looking into the void of human artificiality.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Why do their eyes follow you? Why must they sing in that tinny, soulless voice?

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I can't sleep. Every time I close my eyes, I see their painted faces. The plastic hands. The endless, meaningless laughter.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: They're watching me. Even now. Even when they're turned off, I can feel their synthetic gaze boring into my consciousness. Make it stop. Make it stop!

jokes.text: Hercules! The Cyclops tore off my clothes.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge watching 'Erotic Adventures of Hercules'
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous dialogue from cheesy adult film
jokes.explanation: Payoff to earlier Norman Fell joke with appropriately cheesy dialogue

dialogue.speaker: TV Character
dialogue.line: Oh Hercules, you're so strong and powerful!

dialogue.speaker: TV Character
dialogue.line: And you're so... flexible.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Woo-hoo!

jokes.text: Ahh! I can see the music.
jokes.setup: Lisa having consumed something that's affecting her perception
jokes.punchline: Synesthetic hallucination indicating she's under the influence
jokes.explanation: Standard drug reference humor with economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: The colors are singing and the music smells like purple!

jokes.text: Can't you do something? Hey. Surly only looks out for one guy-- Surly. Sorry, Surly. Shut up.
jokes.setup: Selma asking Surly for help with emergency
jokes.punchline: Surly's selfish response followed by talking to himself in third person
jokes.explanation: Perfect Surly character moment - third-person selfishness with self-correction

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Surly, I need your help with an emergency!

dialogue.speaker: Surly Duff
dialogue.line: Sorry lady, Surly's got his own problems. Surly don't help nobody but Surly.

jokes.text: I think their father is missing a chromosome.
jokes.setup: Selma explaining kids' behavior to Duff Gardens security
jokes.punchline: Her genetic insult about Homer's intelligence
jokes.explanation: Standard intelligence insult with scientific specificity from Selma

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Well, you see, kids can be unpredictable. They get excited, they run around... it's just their nature.

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Of course, some of them inherit stupidity from their parents. Like my sister Marge's husband Homer — that man has the intelligence of a concrete block.

jokes.text: I am the Lizard Queen!
jokes.setup: Lisa found swimming naked in Fermentarium, clearly affected by something
jokes.punchline: Her grandiose declaration of reptilian royalty
jokes.explanation: Iconic Simpsons quote - Lisa's drug-induced delusion became culturally memorable

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I am the Lizard Queen! I am the Lizard Queen!

jokes.text: Oh, I'm not a doctor.
jokes.setup: Worker giving medical advice and medication to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of lack of medical qualifications
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about theme park medical negligence - casual admission of unqualified treatment

dialogue.speaker: Duff Gardens Worker
dialogue.line: Here, take two of these and call me in the morning.

dialogue.speaker: Duff Gardens Worker
dialogue.line: Oh, I should mention — I'm not a real doctor. I'm just an actor.

jokes.text: Come to Homercles! I can't. The beans will burn. Homercles cares not for beans.
jokes.setup: Homer hallucinating that he's Hercules while sick
jokes.punchline: His mythological persona dismissing domestic concerns
jokes.explanation: 'Homercles' wordplay with heroic dismissal of mundane kitchen duties

dialogue.speaker: Homer (as Hercules)
dialogue.line: I am Hercules! I don't do chores!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: But Homer, the garbage needs to go out.

dialogue.speaker: Homer (as Hercules)
dialogue.line: That is a task for mortals, not a god!

jokes.text: You take an ordinary bed sheet, fold it around like this-- No. I mean raising kids.
jokes.setup: Selma asking Homer how he does 'it' (raises kids)
jokes.punchline: Homer misunderstanding and explaining bed-making technique
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection joke with Homer's literal interpretation

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: How do you do it, Homer? How do you raise three kids?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, first you gotta get the fitted sheet on the corners. Then you pull it tight so there's no wrinkles.

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: Homer, I'm asking about raising children!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh! Well, same technique really.

jokes.text: When I went to pick him up, Mom was trying to stab him with a hat pin.
jokes.setup: Selma talking about taking care of JubJub the iguana
jokes.punchline: Revealing Grandma Simpson was trying to kill the pet
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about elderly person's hostility toward exotic pet

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: I've been taking such good care of JubJub. I feed him, I clean his cage, I make sure he's comfortable.

dialogue.speaker: Grandma Simpson
dialogue.line: That's nice, dear.

dialogue.speaker: Selma
dialogue.line: In fact, I found him unconscious this morning with a pillow over his face.

dialogue.speaker: Grandma Simpson
dialogue.line: Must've rolled over in his sleep.
